mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. A sustained monthly decline in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over the preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% as of October 2010 suggests elevated slack in labor market
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines exceed 0.2pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales signal strengthening consumer demand and economic momentum, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. A month-over-month increase in RSAFS of more than 0.5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to improved demand signals.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 373,103 million as of October 2010
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS increases exceed 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero combined with elevated inflation expectations creates a negative real interest rate environment that reduces the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to bonds. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 1.5% annualized.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 218.372 as of November 2010
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with low real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and annualized CPIAUCSL growth > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate earnings growth. A quarterly increase in INDPRO of more than 1.5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as markets recognize improving operational efficiency and output growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases by more than 1.5% over the preceding 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO increases exceed 1.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals weakening construction demand and reduced future consumer wealth effects from housing appreciation. When HOUST falls below 600 thousand units annualized, equity valuations compress due to lower expected future consumption and corporate earnings. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 600 thousand units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 610.0 thousand units as of September 2010, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: post-housing-crisis recovery with structural demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where HOUST < 600k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with declining unemployment creates a virtuous labor market cycle that boosts consumer confidence and spending power. When PAYEMS increases by more than 150 thousand persons month-over-month AND UNRATE declines simultaneously, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days due to improved earnings visibility from stronger consumer demand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150k MoM while UNRATE declines by at least 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,462 thousand and UNRATE at 9.6% as of October 2010
regime_hint: labor market recovery with synchronized employment and unemployment improvement
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 serves as the fundamental driver of long-term equity valuations. When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 60 trading days as markets price in sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,260.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2010
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of quarters where annualized GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% creates a regime of negative real rates that erodes bond returns and forces capital into equities. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when both conditions hold simultaneously, as the opportunity cost of equities becomes negative relative to fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 218.372 as of November 2010
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with monetary accommodation and rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both CPIAUCSL annualized growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
